entries.label: Plugin Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined configuration payload interpreted by that plugin's own schema and validation rules. Use only documented fields from the plugin to prevent ignored or invalid settings.

entries.label: Plugin Enabled
entries.help: Per-plugin enablement override for a specific entry, applied on top of global plugin policy (restart required). Use this to stage plugin rollout gradually across environments.

entries.label: Plugin Hook Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin typed hook policy controls for core-enforced safety gates. Use this to constrain high-impact hook categories without disabling the entire plugin.

entries.label: Allow Conversation Access Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may read raw conversation content from typed hooks such as `llm_input`, `llm_output`, `before_agent_finalize`, and `agent_end`. Non-bundled plugins must opt in explicitly.

entries.label: Allow Prompt Injection Hooks
entries.help: Controls whether this plugin may mutate prompts through typed hooks. Set false to block `before_prompt_build` and ignore prompt-mutating fields from legacy `before_agent_start`, while preserving legacy `modelOverride` and `providerOverride` behavior.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Policy
entries.help: Per-plugin subagent runtime controls for model override trust and allowlists. Keep this unset unless a plugin must explicitly steer subagent model selection.

entries.label: Plugin Subagent Allowed Models
entries.help: Allowed override targets for trusted plugin subagent runs as canonical "provider/model" refs. Use "*" only when you intentionally allow any model.

entries.label: Allow Plugin Subagent Model Override
entries.help: Explicitly allows this plugin to request provider/model overrides in background subagent runs. Keep false unless the plugin is trusted to steer model selection.

entries.label: ACPX Runtime
entries.help: Embedded ACP runtime backend with plugin-owned session and transport management. (plugin: acpx)

entries.label: ACPX Runtime Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined config payload for acpx.

entries.label: Agent Commands
entries.help: Optional per-agent command overrides for the embedded ACP runtime.

entries.label: Default Working Directory
entries.help: Default working directory for embedded ACP session operations when not set per session.

entries.label: MCP Servers
entries.help: Named MCP server definitions to inject into embedded ACP session bootstrap. Each entry needs a command and can include args and env.

entries.label: Non-Interactive Permission Policy
entries.help: Policy when interactive permission prompts are unavailable.

entries.label: Kova Tools MCP Bridge
entries.help: Default off. When enabled, inject the built-in Kova core-tools MCP server into embedded ACP sessions so ACP agents can call selected built-in tools such as cron.

entries.label: Permission Mode
entries.help: Default permission policy for embedded ACP runtime prompts.

entries.label: Plugin Tools MCP Bridge
entries.help: Default off. When enabled, inject the built-in Kova plugin-tools MCP server into embedded ACP sessions so ACP agents can call plugin-registered tools.

entries.label: Health Probe Agent
entries.help: Agent id used for the embedded ACP runtime health probe. Defaults to the first `acp.allowedAgents` entry when that allowlist is set, otherwise to the runtime built-in probe agent (codex). Set this explicitly (for example `opencode` or `claude`) when the default probe agent is not installed or not authenticated, so the whole embedded ACP backend does not get marked unavailable.

entries.label: Queue Owner TTL Seconds
entries.help: Reserved compatibility field for the older embedded ACPX queue-owner path. Accepted for compatibility and logged as ignored.

entries.label: State Directory
entries.help: Directory used for embedded ACP session state and persistence.

entries.label: Strict Windows cmd Wrapper
entries.help: Legacy compatibility field. The current embedded acpx/runtime package uses its own Windows command resolution behavior. Setting this to false is accepted for compatibility and logged as ignored.

entries.label: Prompt Timeout Seconds
entries.help: Timeout for each embedded runtime turn. Defaults to 120 seconds so slower Gemini CLI ACP startups have room to initialize.

entries.label: Active Memory
entries.help: Runs a bounded blocking memory sub-agent before eligible conversational replies and injects relevant memory into prompt context. (plugin: active-memory)

entries.label: Active Memory Config
entries.help: Plugin-defined config payload for active-memory.

entries.label: Target Agents
entries.help: Explicit agent ids that may use active memory.

entries.label: Allowed Chat Types
entries.help: Choose which session types may run Active Memory. Defaults to direct-message style sessions only.

entries.label: Active Memory Recall
entries.help: Globally enable or pause Active Memory recall while keeping the plugin command available.

entries.label: Enable Logging
entries.help: Emit active memory timing and result logs.

entries.label: Max Summary Characters
entries.help: Maximum total characters allowed in the active-memory summary.

entries.label: Memory Model
entries.help: Provider/model used for the blocking memory sub-agent.

entries.label: Fallback Memory Model
entries.help: Optional provider/model to use if no explicit plugin model, session model, or agent primary model resolves.

entries.label: Model Fallback Policy
entries.help: Deprecated compatibility field. Active Memory no longer uses a built-in fallback model; set modelFallback explicitly if you want a fallback.

entries.label: Persist Transcripts
entries.help: Keep blocking memory sub-agent session transcripts on disk in a separate plugin-owned directory.

entries.label: Prompt Append
entries.help: Advanced: append extra operator instructions after the default Active Memory sub-agent instructions.

entries.label: Prompt Override
entries.help: Advanced: replace the default Active Memory sub-agent instructions. Conversation context is still appended.

entries.label: Prompt Style
entries.help: Choose how eager or strict the blocking memory sub-agent should be when deciding whether to return memory.

entries.label: QMD Search Mode
entries.help: Override the QMD search mode used by the blocking memory sub-agent. Defaults to fast lexical search; use inherit to match the main memory backend setting.

entries.label: Query Mode
entries.help: Choose whether the blocking memory sub-agent sees only the latest user message, a small recent tail, or the full conversation.

entries.label: Thinking Override
entries.help: Advanced: optional thinking level for the blocking memory sub-agent. Defaults to off for speed.

entries.label: Transcript Directory
entries.help: Relative directory under the agent sessions folder used when transcript persistence is enabled.

entries.label: @openclaw/amazon-bedrock-provider Config

entries.label: Model Discovery
entries.help: Plugin-owned controls for Amazon Bedrock model auto-discovery.

entries.label: Default Context Window
entries.help: Fallback context window to assign to discovered Bedrock models.

entries.label: Default Max Tokens
entries.help: Fallback max output tokens to assign to discovered Bedrock models.

entries.label: Enable Discovery
entries.help: When false, Kova keeps the Amazon Bedrock plugin available but skips implicit startup discovery. When true, discovery can run even without AWS auth env markers.

entries.label: Provider Filter
entries.help: Optional Bedrock provider-name allowlist for discovery, such as anthropic or amazon.

entries.label: Discovery Refresh Interval (s)
entries.help: How long to cache Bedrock discovery results in seconds. Set to 0 to disable caching.

entries.label: Discovery Region
entries.help: AWS region to use for Bedrock model discovery. Defaults to AWS_REGION, AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, then us-east-1.

entries.label: Guardrail
entries.help: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails settings applied to Bedrock model invocations.
